skill.name: plan
skill.description: Guidelines for planning complex coding tasks before implementation, including exploration, clarifying questions, approval, and verification steps.
skill.summary: Plan complex coding work with clear scope, questions, approval, and verification.
skill.author: 0xmsc

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged command references, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found documentation text rather than executable behavior. The only remaining risk factor is low severity: the skill advises basic repository exploration commands before planning.

low_findings.title: Benign Command-Line Exploration Guidance
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported four medium external command findings at the same line. The line is Markdown guidance to use common exploration tools such as ls, grep, find, and read_file before planning. This is not executable code and shows no command injection or persistence behavior. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for command execution risk. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The referenced line contains only documentation text and no runnable script, dynamic arguments, or automatic execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line contains only documentation text and no runnable script, dynamic arguments, or automatic execution path.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged high severity weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md line 3 and references/principles.md line 8. The text discusses implementation strategy and design planning, not DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic operation. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The exact locations contain planning prose, with no crypto API calls, hashes, keys, or security-sensitive transformations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact locations contain planning prose, with no crypto API calls, hashes, keys, or security-sensitive transformations.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a low severity system reconnaissance pattern at references/principles.md line 9. The line advises avoiding excessive planning time and does not request host inventory, secret discovery, network enumeration, or data exfiltration. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: The line is ordinary process guidance and contains no instruction to collect sensitive system details or transmit results.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is ordinary process guidance and contains no instruction to collect sensitive system details or transmit results.

content.value_statement: Complex coding tasks can fail when scope, risks, and verification are unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create concise plans before implementation.
actual_capabilities: Structures plans around objective, approach, steps, and verification.
Prompts codebase exploration before proposing implementation work.
Requires clarifying questions with proposed answers for complex tasks.
Separates planning from implementation until user approval.
Highlights risky changes and practical verification steps.
limitations: It provides planning guidance, not domain-specific architecture decisions.
It depends on accurate repository exploration by the assistant.
It can add unnecessary overhead for very small tasks.
It does not enforce approval outside the assistant workflow.

use_cases.title: Plan a multi-file feature
use_cases.description: Create a scoped implementation plan before changing shared application code.

use_cases.title: Review implementation risk
use_cases.description: Identify risky changes, dependencies, and verification steps before approving work.

use_cases.title: Clarify ambiguous requests
use_cases.description: Turn broad product requests into concrete milestones, questions, and expected outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a single change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plan skill to create an implementation plan for this task. Include objective, approach, steps, and verification.

prompt_templates.title: Ask clarifying questions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plan skill to identify the key unknowns. Provide at least three clarifying questions with proposed answers.

prompt_templates.title: Assess implementation risk
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare for a risky or shared code change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plan skill to review the risky parts of this change. Include mitigation steps and verification for each risk.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an incremental rollout
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a larger effort.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plan skill to break this work into approved milestones. Include dependencies, decision points, rollback checks, and verification.

output_examples.input: Plan a refactor that changes shared authentication behavior.
output: Objective: Refactor authentication without changing user-visible login behavior.
Approach: Inspect existing flows, identify shared utilities, and change one path at a time.
Verification: Run authentication tests, check failed login behavior, and test a successful session manually.

output_examples.input: Plan a new reporting dashboard from an unclear request.
output: Clarifying questions: Which metrics are required, who uses the dashboard, and what refresh interval is expected?
Proposed answers: Start with daily metrics for operations users and confirm before implementation.
Next step: Draft the smallest useful milestone after repository exploration.

output_examples.input: Plan a large migration with deployment risk.
output: Milestone 1: Inventory current dependencies and owners.
Milestone 2: Add compatibility tests before changing behavior.
Risk mitigation: Keep rollback steps and verification checks visible for each milestone.

best_practices: Inspect the repository before proposing implementation steps.
Keep plans short enough for the user to review quickly.
Tie every risky change to a clear verification step.
anti_patterns: Starting implementation before the plan is approved.
Using vague steps that cannot be verified.
Ignoring existing project conventions during planning.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps assistants create clear implementation plans before changing code.

faq.question: Does it write code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides planning and approval before implementation begins.

faq.question: Why does it ask clarifying questions?
faq.answer: Clarifying questions reduce incorrect assumptions and help users approve a realistic plan.

faq.question: Can it handle large projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends iterative milestones instead of one oversized plan.

faq.question: Is repository access required?
faq.answer: Repository context is strongly recommended so the plan matches existing files and conventions.

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
